title: HTML Tokenization, Tree Construction, goquery, and Parser Differentials · Language Lens: Node Work Budget
summary: Source markup is treated as browser DOM, tokenizer and tree APIs are mixed, goquery is assumed to run JavaScript, malformed HTML repair is never tested. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go node work budget increment in budgeted Go HTML tree adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Bound input bytes, tokens, nodes, depth, attributes, text, selector work, and parse time with explicit rejection.
Compare Go tree results with controlled browser DOM for risk-bearing cases and classify every material difference.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
